reviews.author: Markus Lambricht
reviews.text: Even though I didn’t spot any crocodiles during my visit, the trip to Crocodile Lake was absolutely worth it. The combination of the bike ride and the subsequent hike through the jungle was a great experience. Once you reach the lake, the observation platforms are perfect for a break—the view is beautiful and the silence is simply heavenly. A must-do for nature lovers who enjoy a bit of activity and tranquility!

reviews.author: David Sheppard
reviews.text: The best place in the park to see birds, crocodile, snakes, lizards etc. Nice enough hotel with OK meals. Love it here.

reviews.author: Renzo Iten
reviews.text: Very beautiful wetland area with a well built out hiking path there. And nost importantly: impressive crocodiles!

reviews.author: Alec
reviews.text: Must see if you are in Cat Tien. Started early in the day by crossing the river which costed around 60k/person. We then paid the ticket admissions for the crocodile lake at the HQ building. Please make sure you do this before making your way to the lake. It costs around 250k/person. Then went to rent bicycles which are 150k/person for 1 day (excl. Deposit) Recommend for 1 day instead of half as you want to spend some time at the crocodile lake. We cycled 9km to the start of the jungle path. There are signs for crocodile lake as well as bike racks to leave your bicycle. The bike journey takes between 45min - 1hr There you will be required to show your admission tickets. The walk along the jungle path is approx. 5km (little less from my garmin recording) one way. The route is very clear and well marked. Does not require a guide. Not much elevation and takes between 1-1.5hr depending on level of fitness. There are viewing platforms and services at the lake for refreshments and bathroom breaks. Make sure to spray lots of mosquito repellent. Overall highly recommend 👍

reviews.author: matt
reviews.text: Get a guide for the walk, they will point out wildlife you wouldn't otherwise see. We Also got a lunch thrown in, including a fish the rangers assured me they had to fight a crocodile for! Got within 6m of a crocodile on the waters edge, who was clearly hoping I would come closer.

reviews.author: Tanya Kelm
reviews.text: A lovely day in the park - Cat Tien - I recommend renting bikes and biking around. You can stay at croc lake too, but pretty basic amenities and lots of added fees they have. It was nice and peaceful. If you’re coming to cat Tien it’s the main thing to do! Stayed at Cat Tien Backpackers and it’s around the corner, so highly recommend that as well!

reviews.author: Megan Danielle
reviews.text: Not worth the 10km cycle then 5km (10km both ways) walk, or the 250k dong entrance fee, there is no information about this fee at the enterance gate. There's a small wooden walkway with a cafe and only some seating further on. A few large crocs and some other water based birds to look at which was cool but only good thing about it. I went into the cafe just to get a better view of the crocs and was expected to buy something. Just a tourist trap that's way over priced for what it is. Expect a headache on the way there from all the screaming bugs.

reviews.author: Denis Yanov
reviews.text: The lake itself is nothing special, if you are lucky to see crocodiles then those 250k dongs are not wasted. It is pretty expensive for just to see a lake and maybe some heads of the crocodiles. Beware of bloodsucking leaches that will hunt you all the way to the lake (5km of walking). The path to the lake and 400 years tree are better than the lake itself.

reviews.author: Elisa Pandini
reviews.text: It’s not really worth the price you pay. It’s a nice and quiet lake, but there is nothing to do around there a part of having some food or drinks at the range. We maybe expected too much, but we were a bit disappointed. We did see two crocodiles that came to the surface for a few seconds to breathe, but that’s kind of it. We also found the path to get there pretty boring, especially the bike path. Something we are super glad of is that walking to the lake we bumped into a small group of gibbons that were eating on the threes, but the locals told us it’s not usual to see them on that path and that we were lucky, so do not expect to see them, but do keep ears and eyes open. :) Warning: on the walking path there are many leeches that will try to climb up you shoes. Wear thick socks and long trousers!

reviews.author: flo Turtle
reviews.text: 60,000 each to cross the river by ferry and access the national park. We hired bikes for 150,000 for 8 hours and there were plenty of trails you could cycle around. We decided to go to the crocodile lake, which cost an extra 250,000 to access. You have to walk 5km to and from the lake from where you leave the bikes (9km cycle to and from entrance to park). Also see a HUGE tree en route. Well maintained trails and not wet at all when we went (April). Saw a croc up close, munching on some fish which was an incredible experience and definitely worth every penny in my opinion.

reviews.author: Tâm Phan Minh
reviews.text: Such a paradise for wildlife animal. You can see beautiful bird as Green Peafowl, Hornbill, Waterbirds,... Some animals as Crocodile, Muntjac, Gaur, Sambar deer in this place

reviews.author: Hong Lien Tran
reviews.text: This is a paradise for the wild animal. I recommend staying here overnight as we saw more activities of nightlife than the normal day. Also, there are numerous stories from local offices who have remained there for decades.

reviews.author: Saltanat Zhakenova
reviews.text: Nice place! We bought the tour from the hotel. It provided the jeep and an English-speaking tour guide from the national park. She showed us primates, snakes, flying lizards, insects with amazing camouflage, and both small and large spiders. The biodiversity is incredible, but without a guide, you wouldn’t be able to spot everything. We saw the giant beautiful Tunga tree, more than six large crocodiles, one of which was around 4 meters long, along with her six small baby crocodiles. It was an unforgettable journey—highly recommended!

reviews.author: Lauren Hodgetts
reviews.text: Fantastic wildlife watching. A beautiful hike and bike ride to get here with ample gibbon and Langur sightings. By the lake you can see lots of the endemic crocodile from a nice watch point. We saw lots of lovely birds including more rare species like king fishers, hawks etc. Overall the national park is beautiful with lots of old grand trees, a nice trail and some of the best wildlife we saw in SEA. After the bike ride or car ride, it is a 5km walk.There are toilets at the watch tower too, the staff were also helpful in providing us info books about the national park and animal ID books.

reviews.author: Jeff Chane-mouye
reviews.text: Come and see the crocodiles bit also the langurs!

reviews.author: Daniel Stork
reviews.text: Beautiful, serene place with great bird watching and pretty good lunch. Also included some rooms and the fair share of crocodiles reintroduced with support of the Dutch government.

reviews.author: Fiona Audsley
reviews.text: Beautiful tranquil spot in cat tien national park

reviews.author: Valenor
reviews.text: 2 big crocodiles ran out of the water towards us after a guy threw some fish. Great place

reviews.author: Florian Remy
reviews.text: Quick summary for future visitors : - You will have to purchase an entry ticket at 60k VND/person in order to take the ferry which will lead you to the park. - Once you have crossed the river, on your left you will see the building where you can buy the ticket to the crocodile lake. The cost is 250k VND/person. They can also arrange a car for you if you like, your other options being walking (9km) or cycling. I'd recommend the lastest option : cycling. - If you decide to cycle, the place with all the bicycles is in front of the building where you bought the tickets for the crocodiles. The cost is 150k VND/bike, and they might ask 100k VND of deposit. - Be prepared for being surrounded by leeches once you are on the 5km footway to the crocodiles lake. I'd highly recommend wearing a trouser, boots, thick socks and a hat. I was wearing a short with running shoes, and even though I walked fast and didn't stop, they managed to climb on my shoes and I got a lot of bites. Not particularly painful, but not pleasant either. As others have said before, the place is great. You get to enjoy the feeling of being in the jungle (lots of insects and animals sounds, the rapids along the way) with the comfort of a concrete bicycle path. The crocodile lake is stunning and the people working there are very friendly. The view on the lake from their house is stunning, it feels like the time has stopped.

reviews.author: Sarah Jacobs
reviews.text: Cycled 10km to Crocodile Lake to find out it costs 200k cash only each entry fee - we didn't have enough cash on us 😭

reviews.author: Rokas Kalytis
reviews.text: not worth it, even if you stay overnight you might see a glimpse of a croc for a split second

reviews.author: Jack O'Sullivan
reviews.text: Unique experience sleeping with the park rangers on Crocodile lake

reviews.author: Jakub Specjalski
reviews.text: Nice place but a bit too expensive ticket for what it is.

reviews.author: Natalie Cohen
reviews.text: It was a very hot day (37° celsius) so we only got to see some crocs smoothly moving along the shallows, but the place is beautiful, the walk was amazing (with the guide we took pointing out the various flora and fauna, explaining the sounds, etc.). Our friends came on a slightly cooler day and they said the crocodiles even came out of the water! There are restrooms and a restaurant when you get to the lake, though you are in no way obliged to order anything in order to sit and watch the lake, the birds and the crocodiles. We brought a packed lunch from our lodge, but the food other people ordered looked good, and the drinks we ordered were great. Especially the iced coffee with milk (ca phe sua da) which had chocolaty flavours. The boat ride that you can take is pleasant, but I am not sure if there is any extra value other than a pleasant ride. Altogether, a worthwhile half day.

reviews.author: Binh Arsenal
reviews.text: Bạn nên dưỡng sức để đi bộ vào Trạm 5km và đi bộ ra đường xe chạy 5km tổng cộng 10km đường rừng Cát Tiên. Trên đường đi có thể khám phá cây Tung cổ thụ 400 năm và các loại động thực vật đa dạng. Nếu bạn đi đoàn đông muốn ăn trưa hoặc tối, có thể gọi điện thoại nhờ các anh Kiểm lâm Bàu Sấu chuẩn bị với giá cá phải chăng. Cà phê các anh pha rất ngon và view ngắm cá sấu cũng chill. Nên đến thử ít nhất 1 lần để cảm nhận thiên nhiên đặc sắc!

reviews.author: Charlotte E.
reviews.text: Wir sind mit dem Fahrrad (150K VND/Tag + 100K VND Kaution) 10km bis zum Wegbeginn gefahren, die Straße ist befestigt undnleicht befahrbar, es gibt eine Toilette auf der Hälfte des Weges und Snackstände. Dann sind es 5km durch den Dschungel zu Fuß, alles ist im Schatten und einfach zu gehen. Am See haben wir mehrere Krokodile gesehen, da es heiß war waren alle im Wasser. Am See könnte man Getränke kaufen & es gab eine Toilette. Uns hat der Ausflug sehr gefallen, wir sind um 8 Uhr in der Früh an einem Samstag gestartet, es waren relativ wenig Tourist*innen auf diesem Weg.

reviews.author: Chu Chu
reviews.text: Trải nghiệm rất tuyệt vời, mình đã ở trong này với 20 tiếng không có sóng điện thoại ,chỉ có tiếng chim kêu, tiếng gió thổi, tiếng bước chân và mọi thứ rất đáng giá. Các anh kiểm lâm thân thiện và nấu ăn ngon.
